title: Salisbury Steak
intro: Classic American diner Salisbury steak was built on technique, not luxury. Crumbs stretched protein, seasoning carried flavor, and pan heat created crust. This version honors the classic while tightening the details. The patties hold their shape, the crust stays crisp, and the gravy finishes the dish instead of soaking into it.

items.prep: squeezed dry and minced very fine
just enough to lightly coat the pan

stepGroups.title: Rehydrate the mushrooms
items: Cover the porcini and shiitake with hot water or stock. Cover and steep 25 to 30 minutes. Drain, squeeze very dry, and mince extremely fine until nearly paste-like. Discard soaking liquid.

stepGroups.title: Hydrate the binder
items: In a large bowl, whisk the egg, soy sauce, Dijon, and Worcestershire. Add the 35 g panko and stir. Rest 1 to 2 minutes so the crumbs hydrate.

stepGroups.title: Build the patty mix
items: Add Beyond Beef, the minced mushroom paste, chopped onion, smoked paprika, cayenne, garlic powder, sezonador total, parsley, and black pepper. Mix gently just until cohesive. Do not knead.

stepGroups.title: Rest
items: Let the mixture rest 5 minutes in the refrigerator before shaping. This allows the breadcrumbs to fully hydrate and helps the patties hold together cleanly.

stepGroups.title: Shape
items: Form 12 round patties, approximately 3 oz each. Flatten slightly to about ¾ inch thick. Keep edges clean and thickness even.

stepGroups.title: Firm for structure
items: Place patties on a tray and freeze 30 minutes. This step locks geometry, improves crust adhesion, and prevents spreading.

stepGroups.title: Prepare the crust station
items: Whisk cornstarch and cold water into a thin slurry. Place panko in a shallow dish and season lightly with black pepper.

stepGroups.title: Crust the patties
items: Dip each chilled patty quickly in slurry, coating both sides. Let excess drip off so only a thin film remains. Press lightly into panko to coat both sides and edges. You want a shell, not armor.

stepGroups.title: Set the Crust
items: Place the coated patties back on the tray and let them rest 5 minutes in the refrigerator before frying. This helps the coating adhere and reduces crumb loss in the pan.

stepGroups.title: Warm the gravy
items: Heat the Heinz jar gravy gently in a small saucepan. Keep warm but do not boil.

stepGroups.title: Pan-fry
items: Heat a cast-iron or stainless skillet over medium heat. Add a thin film of olive oil. Fry patties 3 to 4 minutes per side, flipping once, until deeply golden and fully set. Cook in batches if necessary. Do not crowd.

stepGroups.title: Serve
items: Plate patties first. Spoon hot gravy over the top only. Do not simmer crusted patties in gravy. Keep the crust crisp and let the gravy work on the plate.
For the full TV dinner experience, serve with boxed instant mashed potatoes, prepared with olive oil and skim milk instead of butter and whole milk.

stepGroups.title: Note:
items: Do not use nut milk for the potatoes. Even unsweetened nut milks tend to make the potatoes taste sweet.
